feature: Derive the left-sidebar tree from page URLs under `sidebar.base`, with no curated _data/navigation/*.yml file.
route: /docs/wargames/bandit/bandit5/  (a consumer site with 219 vendored OverTheWire pages under /docs/wargames/, config `sidebar: {nav: pages, base: /docs/wargames/, order_by: nav_order}`)
measurement: counts under `aside.bd-sidebar` in the raw server-rendered HTML (no client JS): section groups, nav-tree links, child (level) links, active link, expanded (`.collapse.show`) sections

before.note: `nav: pages` is unrecognized, so the content gate is false and the left navigation column is not rendered at all. Getting a browsable tree required hand-authoring/generating a ~219-entry _data/navigation/wargames.yml.

how_produced: Two real Jekyll builds of the same fixture site (219 pages, identical config). BEFORE loads the theme from an origin/main git worktree; AFTER loads it from this PR's working tree. Both built with an empty baseurl and served locally; counts and screenshots captured with Playwright/Chromium at 1280px.
